description: Tabbed Runner UI with Claude Code Interaction - Replace single-pane UI with a tab-based layout supporting multiple simultaneous workflow runners, each with live log output and stdin input.

tasks.title: Runner tab data model
tasks.description: As a developer, I need a data structure to hold per-runner state so that multiple runners can coexist without sharing mutable fields.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a `RunnerTab` struct in `app.rs` with fields: `workflow_name: String`, `log_lines: Vec<String>` (capped at 1000 lines, oldest dropped), `state: RunnerTabState` (enum: `Running { iteration: u32 }`, `Done`, `Error(String)`), `runner_rx: Option<UnboundedReceiver<RunnerEvent>>`, `runner_kill_tx: Option<oneshot::Sender<()>>`, `stdin_tx: Option<UnboundedSender<String>>`, `input_buffer: String`, `log_scroll: usize`
Remove flat `log_lines`, `runner_rx`, `runner_kill_tx`, and `app_state` fields from `App`; runner-specific state lives inside `RunnerTab`
Add `runner_tabs: Vec<RunnerTab>` and `active_tab: usize` (0 = Workflows tab) to `App`
Typecheck passes

tasks.title: Pipe stdin to the claude subprocess
tasks.description: As a developer, I need the claude subprocess to have a writable stdin pipe so the user's typed responses can be delivered to it.
acceptanceCriteria: `runner_task` gains a `stdin_rx: UnboundedReceiver<String>` parameter
`Command::stdin(Stdio::piped())` is used; `child.stdin.take()` gives a writable handle
A dedicated Tokio task inside `runner_task` reads lines from `stdin_rx` and writes them (appending `\n`) to the child's stdin handle using `tokio::io::AsyncWriteExt`
If the stdin channel closes or the write fails, the stdin task exits silently
The corresponding `UnboundedSender<String>` is stored in `RunnerTab::stdin_tx`
Typecheck passes

tasks.title: Tab bar rendering
tasks.description: As a user, I want to see all open tabs at the top of the screen so I know what is available and which tab is active.
acceptanceCriteria: A single-line tab bar is drawn at the very top of the terminal
Tab 0 is always labelled `[Workflows]`
Each runner tab is labelled `[<workflow-name>]` while running, `[<workflow-name> ✓]` when Done, and `[<workflow-name> !]` on error
The active tab is highlighted (reversed/bold style)
If tabs overflow terminal width, visible tabs are cropped (no scrolling required)
The rest of the screen below the tab bar renders the content of the active tab
Typecheck passes
Verify visually in the running TUI

tasks.title: Tab navigation with t-prefix chords
tasks.description: As a user, I want to switch tabs with `t` + a digit or `t` + an arrow key so I can jump to any tab without leaving the keyboard home row.
acceptanceCriteria: Pressing `t` sets `tab_nav_pending: bool` on `App` and waits for the next keypress

While `tab_nav_pending` is true: digit `1`–`9` switches to tab at index digit − 1 (out-of-range ignored); `Left`/`Right` arrow cycles to prev/next tab (wrapping); any other key clears the flag with no tab change
`tab_nav_pending` is cleared after any resolution (successful or not)
When active tab changes, the previous tab's input focus and state are preserved
Typecheck passes

tasks.title: Workflows tab no regression
tasks.description: As a user, I want the Workflows tab to look and behave exactly as before so that existing workflow management is not disrupted.
acceptanceCriteria: When `active_tab == 0`, the full Workflows layout (workflow list left, tasks right, log panel, status bar) is rendered exactly as before
All existing keybindings (`n`, `d`, `r`, `s`, `e`, `?`, `q`, arrows) work normally while the Workflows tab is active
Pressing `r` on a selected workflow starts the runner AND switches `active_tab` to the newly created runner tab
Typecheck passes
Verify visually in the running TUI

tasks.title: Runner tab log view with scroll support
tasks.description: As a user, I want to see the live stdout/stderr of the running claude process in the runner tab so I can follow its progress.
acceptanceCriteria: When active tab is a runner tab, the area below the tab bar is split: log view (available height minus 3 lines) on top, input row on bottom
Log lines are rendered newest-at-bottom (append style)
Log view auto-scrolls to the bottom as new lines arrive unless the user has manually scrolled up
User can scroll the log with `Up`/`Down` or `k`/`j`; scrolling up disables auto-scroll until `End` or `G` re-enables it
Runner tab shows a status line (e.g., `Running — iteration 2/10` or `Done`) above the input box
Typecheck passes
Verify visually in the running TUI

tasks.title: Input field and send-to-claude
tasks.description: As a user, I want a text input field at the bottom of the runner tab where I can type a response and press Enter to send it to Claude Code.
acceptanceCriteria: Bottom row of every active runner tab shows `> ` followed by `input_buffer` content

While a runner tab is active (no dialog open): printable chars append to `input_buffer`; `Backspace` removes last char; `Enter` sends trimmed `input_buffer` via `stdin_tx` then clears it; `Esc` clears `input_buffer` without sending
Scroll keys (`Up`/`Down`, `k`/`j`) scroll the log and do not affect the input buffer
If `stdin_tx` is `None` (runner done), pressing Enter shows a transient status message 'Runner is not active' for 2 s
Typecheck passes
Verify visually in the running TUI: type a message and confirm it is delivered

tasks.title: Runner tab lifecycle: create, done, and close
tasks.description: As a user, I want runner tabs to be created automatically and persist with a Done indicator when the runner exits so I can review output after completion.
acceptanceCriteria: When `start_runner` is called: if a Done/Error tab for that workflow exists it is reused (log cleared, state reset to `Running { iteration: 1 }`); otherwise a new `RunnerTab` is appended; `active_tab` is set to that tab

When runner exits via `RunnerEvent::Exited`: `runner_rx`, `runner_kill_tx`, `stdin_tx` set to `None`; `RunnerTab::state` → `Done` or `Error`; label gains ` ✓` / ` !`; final log line `'--- Runner exited ---'` appended; user stays on runner tab
User can close a Done/Error tab by pressing `x`; `active_tab` moves to previous tab (or 0)
Running tabs cannot be closed with `x`; transient status message 'Stop the runner first [s]' shown for 2 s
ContinuePrompt dialog works per runner tab (shown inside the runner tab)
Typecheck passes
Verify visually in the running TUI
